---
title: "DSHB Professional Development Conference Micro Site Summer 2026"
subtitle: "Tools for Large Scale Data Science"
author: "Dr. Adam Ross Nelson"
---

## Welcome To The Site

Tools come and go. Scientists who will be successful long term in their career are those who can learn tools quickly and independently on their own. Over five days (about 2.5 weeks of this course) students will first work in small groups to learn a new tool and also plan a mini-crash lesson on that tool for peers in class. The first two days of class will be devoted mostly to work time where students can collaborate on learning their assigned tool and plan a 70-minute mini-crash lesson on the dates and times indicated below.

Successful completion of this assignment will likely involve out-of-class meetings in your assigned groups by Google Meet, Zoom, MS Teams, or in person. Each student will individually need to complete one or more multi-hour tutorials. Most of these tool creators have published "Quick Start" guides or video tutorials. A component of this assignment is also learning to navigate those Quick Start guides and video tutorials on your own and to evaluate which are worth your while and which are not.

Additionally, each group will meet with the instructor at least once during class to discuss the tool and the mini-crash course lesson planning approach. After that, each group will meet at least once outside of class with the instructor to further refine, develop, focus, simplify, organize, or rehearse the mini-crash course lesson.

## Conference Schedule

| Time              | June 24, 2026                                                                 | June 25, 2026                                                                                          | July 1, 2026                                                                                                |
|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| 2:00 – 2:10 pm    | Daily reporter reports                                                        | Daily reporter reports                                                                                 | Daily reporter reports                                                                                      |
| 2:10 – 3:20 pm    | [Installing Windsurf and getting started with agentic coding](topics/windsurf.qmd) | [Simple exploratory data analysis with SQL](topics/sql.qmd)                                            | [Using Quarto to create a simple document (resume) + publishing to GitHub Pages](topics/quarto.qmd)         |
| 3:20 – 3:30 pm    | Break                                                                         | Break                                                                                                  | Break                                                                                                       |
| 3:30 – 4:40 pm    | [Installing and running a local LLM with Ollama](topics/ollama.qmd)           | [Refresher on branching, cloning, editing, committing, pushing, and submitting a pull request on GitHub](topics/github.qmd) | [Creating a Qualtrics account and setting up your first survey + tour of question types](topics/qualtrics.qmd) |
| 4:40 – 4:50 pm    | Break                                                                         | Break                                                                                                  | Break                                                                                                       |
| 4:50 – 5:00 pm    | Large group wrap-up                                                           | Large group wrap-up                                                                                    | Large group wrap-up                                                                                         |

Group members for each session: see class handouts.

## In-Class Instructor Consultation Schedule

### June 17, 2026 — Welcome + Course Orientation

- **2:00 – 2:50 pm** — Re-introductions. Course overview. Explanation of the professional development conference format for the first 2.5 weeks of the course. An activity designed to help students identify their own strengths in presenting to or educating others about complex topics.
- **2:50 – 3:00 pm** — Break
- **3:00 – 3:50 pm** — Demonstration mini-crash lesson on the [Command Line Interface + File System As a Tool](topics/cli.qmd)
- **4:00 – 4:20 pm** — Detailed discussion of the six topics, group assignments, and presentation schedule ahead.
- **4:20 – 5:00 pm** — Instructor consultation with the *Installing Windsurf and getting started with agentic coding* group. Other groups work together. Stay together in classroom.

### June 18, 2026 — Continued Instructor Consultations + Small Group Planning Time

- **2:00 – 2:40 pm** — Instructor consultation with the *Installing and running a local LLM with Ollama* group.
- **2:40 – 3:20 pm** — Instructor consultation with the *Simple exploratory data analysis with SQL* group.
- **3:20 – 4:00 pm** — Instructor consultation with the *Refresher on branching, cloning, editing, committing, pushing, and submitting a pull request on GitHub* group.
- **4:00 – 4:40 pm** — Instructor consultation with the *Using Quarto to create a simple document (resume) + publishing to GitHub Pages* group.
- **4:40 – 5:20 pm** — Instructor consultation with the *Creating a Qualtrics account and setting up your first survey + tour of question types* group.\*

During class other groups work together during their non-consultation slots. Stay together
in the classroom.

\* Students in this last group are welcome to arrive at 2:40 pm to compensate
for staying later.

## Out-Of-Class Instructor Consultation Schedule

### June 23, 2026 (For June 24th and June 25th)

- 4:00pm June 23 — Instructor consultation with the *Installing and running a local LLM with Ollama* group.
- 5:00pm June 23 - Instructor consultation with the *Installing and running a local LLM with Ollama* group.
- 6:00pm June 23 - Instructor consultation with the *Simple Exploratory Data Analysis with SQL* group.
- 7:00pm June 23 - Instructor consultation with the *Refresger on branching, cloning, editing, committing, pushing, and submitting a pull request on GitHub* group.

### June 30, 2026 (For July 1st)

- 4:00pm June 30 - Instructor consultation with the *Quarto + GitHub Pages* group.
- 5:00pm June 30 - Instructor consultation with the *Creating a Qualtrics account and setting up your first survey + tour of question types* group.

## Quick Links

- [About this microsite](about.qmd)
- [Site provenance](provenance.qmd)
- [Maintainer guide](maintainer-guide.qmd)
- [Accessibility features](accessibility.qmd)
